title: Boat Rental in Seychelles: How to Choose Your Ideal Vessel in 2026
html:
When you decide to book a boat online in Seychelles, you face a dazzling choice of vessels cruising from Mahé to Praslin and beyond. A half-day motorboat charter typically costs from 7,500 SCR, making it a thrilling way to explore the inner islands. BoatHire24 connects you with owners who list their craft on a trusted worldwide marketplace.

Where to set off in Seychelles

You can launch from Eden Island Marina, Victoria (Mahé), Baie Sainte Anne (Praslin), or even from the jetty at La Digue. These are the main hubs for boat rental. Eden Island Marina is a modern complex with fuelling and provisioning, ideal for a full-day charter. From Mahé, you can head to the Sainte Anne Marine National Park just three nautical miles away.

Praslin’s Baie Sainte Anne gives you quick access to the UNESCO-listed Vallée de Mai and the stunning Anse Lazio. La Digue’s smaller jetty suits smaller motorboats and traditional schooners. Each departure point offers a different flavour of island hopping.

Which boat to rent

Select a boat based on your group size, budget, and the type of experience you want. Here is a rundown of the most popular choices:

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Olympic 490), best for 2-4 people wanting a self-drive adventure to nearby coves; from 6,500 SCR for a half-day. Consider this speedboat for a nimble option.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), stable and spacious for 8-10 guests on a full-day cruise around the inner islands; from 28,000 SCR.
  3. Luxury yacht (e.g. Azimut 55), a crewed option for up to 12 people, with flybridge and cabins; from 70,000 SCR per day.
  4. Traditional schooner, a sailing experience for 6-8 guests, often including lunch; from 12,000 SCR per person for a sunset cruise.
  5. Rigid inflatable (RIB), fast and fun for 4-6 people, ideal for reaching remote snorkelling spots; from 4,000 SCR per hour.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Seychelles?

Prices vary widely: a shared snorkelling tour costs around 2,000 SCR per person, while a private motorboat half-day starts at 6,500 SCR. Use book a boat online to browse real-time availability and compare verified listings. Here is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared tour: 2,000, 3,500 SCR per person for a half-day.
  • Small motorboat (half-day): 6,500, 10,000 SCR for the boat.
  • Catamaran (full-day): 28,000, 45,000 SCR for up to 10 guests.
  • Creëwed luxury yacht (full-day): 60,000, 90,000 SCR including skipper and crew.

These rates usually exclude fuel, which can add 1,500, 3,000 SCR for a half-day motorboat trip. Always confirm what is included with the operator.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Seychelles?

Yes, for self-drive bareboat charters you typically need a recognised boating licence, such as an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. For crewed charters, no licence is needed from you.

A local tip: in Seychelles the best sailing season is from May to September when the southeast trade winds are steady at 15, 20 knots, making for exhilarating passages between islands.

If you are hiring a skipper, they will handle navigation and pilotage. Always check with the operator whether their insurance covers your licence type.

At a glance

  • Price band: 2,000, 90,000 SCR per trip
  • Peak months: December, January and July, August
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours), full-day (8 hours), or multi-day
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests depending on vessel
  • Currency: Seychelles rupee (SCR)

Questions about boat rental in Seychelles

Can I take a boat from Mahé to Praslin?

Yes, many operators offer crossings from Victoria to Baie Sainte Anne, taking about 45 minutes by fast motorboat.

Is it possible to rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, if you hold a valid licence and the operator approves your experience, you can rent bareboat for self-drive exploration.

What is the best month for calm seas in Seychelles?

April and October offer the calmest conditions with light winds, ideal for families and less experienced sailors.

Whether you choose a self-drive speedboat or a crewed catamaran, renting in Seychelles is straightforward with platforms like BoatHire24. To see all available vessels in the archipelago, browse the listings. If you own a boat in Seychelles, you can also list it on the marketplace and reach thousands of travellers. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Seychelles and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
